title: Design System Documentation Spec (DSDS) v0.9.0
description: A machine-readable format for design system documentation. It covers components, design tokens, token groups, themes, foundations, patterns, and guides. A DSDS document holds a single entity or one or more entity groups — named collections of entities. Each entity carries a `kind` discriminator that marks it as a component, token, token-group, theme, foundation, pattern, or guide. Entity schemas are in entities/. Document block schemas are in document-blocks/. Common schemas are in common/.

$schema.description: URI reference to the DSDS JSON Schema for validation.

dsdsVersion.description: The DSDS spec version this document conforms to.

extends.description: Declares that this DSDS document inherits from another DSDS document — usually a core design system. The base document provides the core entities. This document layers on top, adding or extending entities. It supports systems-of-systems architectures, where product- or brand-specific systems inherit from a shared core.

documentBlocks.description: System-level structured documentation for the design system as a whole — its purpose (when to adopt the system), cross-cutting guidelines, and narrative overviews. Each entry is a general document block: a `useCases`, `guidelines`, `sections`, `accessibility`, or `content` block. This is the same `documentBlocks` mechanism entities use, scoped to the system rather than a single artifact. Position signals the scope (root vs. inside an entity).

entityGroups.description: One or more entity groups. Each group is a named collection with a single `entities` array. That array holds any mix of entity types — components, tokens, token groups, themes, foundations, patterns, and guides — in display order. Each entity's `kind` identifies its type. Multiple groups let one DSDS file organize entities into logical sections (e.g., one group for foundations, another for components). Groups may be inlined or referenced through a $ref object that points to another DSDS file.

entity.description: A single entity document — one component, token, token group, theme, foundation, pattern, or guide. Use this instead of `entityGroups` when each entity lives in its own file. The `kind` field on the entity identifies its type.

anyEntity.description: Any DSDS entity — component, token, token group, theme, foundation, pattern, or guide. The required `kind` property identifies the entity type and selects its schema. This is the single entity union. The root `entity` property and each entity group's `entities` array both accept it.

fileRef.description: A JSON Reference to a DSDS entity or entity group defined in another file. Tooling (e.g. json-schema-ref-parser) resolves the reference before validation and merge. The URI should include a JSON Pointer fragment to target a specific value within the referenced file (e.g. './button.dsds.json#/entity', './groups/tokens.dsds.json#/entityGroups/0').

$ref.description: A relative reference to a DSDS file, optionally with a JSON Pointer fragment that identifies the target value within that file (e.g., './button.dsds.json#/entity'). MUST be relative. Absolute paths, protocol-relative references, and scheme-bearing URIs (http:, file:, …) are not permitted. A reference to another system uses `extends.url`. Resolvers MUST detect cycles. They MUST treat unresolvable files or pointers and shape mismatches as fatal. They MUST gate any remote dereferencing behind an allow-list (see the spec's reference-resolution and security rules).

entityGroup.description: A named collection of design system entities. All entities live in a single `entities` array, in display order, mixing entity types freely. Each entity's required `kind` identifies its type, so no per-type arrays are needed. Only the group name is required.

name.description: Human-readable name of the collection (e.g., 'Acme Design System', 'Color Tokens', 'Button Documentation'). This is already a display label — it does NOT have an `identifier` counterpart and need not match a slug pattern.

entities.description: The entities in this group, in display order. Any mix of entity kinds — components, tokens, token groups, themes, foundations, patterns, and guides. Each item's `kind` identifies its type. Items may be inlined or referenced through a $ref object that points to another DSDS file. Order is significant: tools SHOULD keep it for display.
